folio.label: 108r
folio.l: They ben so knyt , there may noon harm betide
And namely vp oon , the wyues syde
¢ This olde knygút Ianuare , of whicú I tolde
Considered hatú , witú Inne his dayes olde
The lusty lyf , the vertuouse quiete
That is in mariage , hony swete
And for hise frendes , oon a day he sent
To tellen hem , the effect , of his entent
Witú face sad , this tale he hatú he~ told
He seide frendes , I am hore and old
And almost god wot , oon pittis brynke
Vpon my soule , somwhat must I thynke
I haue my body , fouly dispended
Blissed be god , that it shal be amended
And that a noon , in al the hast that I can
For I wol be certeyn , a wedded man
Vn to sum Maide , fair and tendre of age
I preye ©ow shapetú , for my mariage
Al sodeynly , for I wol nat a bide
And I wyl fond , to spien of my syde
To whom I may be wedded , hastily
But for as mecúe , as ©e ben moo than I
ñe shuln rather , swicú a thyngö a spien
Than I , and where , me best were to allien
But oo thyngö warne I ©ow , my frendes deere
I wol noon old wyf haue , in no manere
She shal nat passe , sextene ©er certeyn
Olde fyssú and ©onge flessú , wolde I haue fayn
Bet q¶ he is a Pyke , than a PykereÂ
And bet than olde Beef , is the tendre weÂ
I wol no wo~man , thretty ©er of age
It is but benstraw , and gret forage
And eke theise olde wydewes , god it wot
They knowe so meche craft , on wades bot
So meche broken harm , whan hem liste
That witú hem , shuld I neuäe lyue in reste
For sondry scoles , maken subtil clerkes
A wo~man of many scoles , half a clerk is
But certeynly , a ©onge thyngö , moun men gye
Rigút as men moun warme wex , w hondis plye
Wherfore I seye ©ow , pleynly in a clause
I wol noon olde wyf haue , rigút for this cause
For if so were , I had swicú myschaunce
That I in hire , coude haue no plesaunce
Than shuld I lede my lyf , in a voutree
